reasoning: No direct relationship between V and I/R observed. Increase R to see if it affects V.
Observing that increasing R while keeping I constant results in V doubling, suggests a linear relationship between V and R.
Observing that increasing R from 20 to 30 resulted in V from 40 to 60, indicates a proportional relationship between V and R.
Observing that V consistently doubles when R is doubled, suggests a direct proportionality between V and R.
Observing the consistent doubling of V when R is increased by the same factor, supports a direct proportionality between V and R.
